name: Fnb Bank, Inc.
description_short: FNB Bank is a Kentucky-based community bank founded in 1875, offering personal and business banking services with over $740 million in assets.
description_long:
FNB Bank, Inc. was established in 1875 in Mayfield, Kentucky, and has operated as a community bank for nearly 150 years. It ranks as Kentucky's 7th oldest community bank and maintains strong local roots with a commitment to keeping money in the community. The bank serves customers across multiple markets including Graves, Calloway, Marshall, McCracken, and Trigg counties in Kentucky.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal banking products including checking accounts, savings accounts, Health Savings Accounts, CDs, IRAs, home mortgages, and personal loans. For business customers, FNB provides business checking and savings accounts, business debit cards, business and agricultural loans, treasury management services, remote deposit capture, merchant services, and business credit cards. Digital banking services include mobile banking, online banking, digital wallets, bill pay, text banking, and a money management tool called Money Manager.

FNB Bank distinguishes itself through its century-long community presence and demonstrated commitment to local giving—donating over $87,000 to area schools in 2025. The bank emphasizes personalized service through local lenders with flexibility, particularly in agricultural lending. They offer specialized products like Greenlight (financial literacy tool for children) and Your Swipe (savings matching program), and provide customer convenience through a mobile app available on iPhone, iPad, and Android platforms.

As a traditional community bank, FNB Bank's primary strength lies in local relationship banking rather than competitive national pricing or fintech innovation. Customers seeking a bank with deep community ties and personal service will find value here, though those prioritizing cutting-edge digital features or the lowest rates may find better options elsewhere. The bank's asset size ($740M+) places it in the mid-tier community bank category—solid and stable but smaller than regional powerhouses. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
pros: Founded in 1875 with 150-year track record of stability and local community presence
Over $740 million in assets, indicating strong financial strength and stability
Dedicated agricultural lending with local lenders described as having 'greater flexibility and responsiveness'
Comprehensive digital banking platform including mobile app, online banking, bill pay, and money management tools
Community-focused with demonstrated giving ($87,000+ to area schools in 2025) and free community events like document shredding
Personal loan and mortgage services available with local relationship managers
Specialized products like Greenlight for children's financial literacy and Your Swipe savings matching program
cons: Limited geographic footprint—serves only 5 Kentucky counties, not suitable for customers outside service area
No mention of competitive interest rates, fee structures, or how they compare to national banks or online competitors
Website lacks specific APR ranges, minimum deposit requirements, or detailed product terms
Smaller asset base ($740M) compared to regional and national banks, potentially limiting product variety and technology investment
No mention of investment advisory services despite 'Investments' link in navigation menu
best_for: Agricultural business owners and farmers in western Kentucky seeking flexible local lending
Families in Graves, Calloway, Marshall, McCracken, or Trigg counties wanting community banking with personal relationships
Parents interested in teaching children financial literacy through Greenlight and other educational products
Small business owners seeking local treasury management and merchant services support
diagnosis: FNB Bank is best suited for customers in western Kentucky seeking a stable, locally-rooted community bank with personal service and agricultural lending expertise. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—the bank only serves five Kentucky counties, making it unavailable to most consumers outside this region, and limited product transparency on pricing and rates may require direct customer contact for comparison shopping.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
